---
title: "Cross-Validation: DB-Path vs Legacy-Path Assessment"
author: "nemascan-sims-nf"
format:
  html:
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 3
    code-fold: false
    code-summary: "Show code"
    embed-resources: true
params:
  existing_assessment: ""
  db_assessment: ""
  db_dir: ""
execute:
  eval: true
  warning: true
  message: true
---

## 1. Overview

This notebook compares the DB-path assessment (`db_simulation_assessment_results.tsv`) against the
legacy-path assessment (`simulation_assessment_results.tsv`) to verify that the DB-path QTL analysis
reproduces the legacy pipeline's analytical results.

It mirrors the 7 tests in `tests/testthat/test-assessment_cross_validation.R` but presents results
as data frames and inline messages instead of pass/fail dots, making it easier to debug mismatches.

### Prerequisites

Run the pipeline with **both** paths enabled:

```bash
NXF_VER=24.10.4 nextflow run main.nf -profile test,docker --legacy_assess
```

Then render with the three path parameters:

```bash
quarto render tests/notebooks/assessment_cross_validation.qmd \
  -P existing_assessment=/path/to/simulation_assessment_results.tsv \
  -P db_assessment=/path/to/db_simulation_assessment_results.tsv \
  -P db_dir=/path/to/output/db
```

Or set them interactively in the `paths` cell before running.

### Known Design Differences

| Aspect | Legacy path | DB path |
|--------|-------------|---------|
| `var.exp` / `Simulated.QTL.VarExp` | Computed from genotype matrix | `NA` (no genotype matrix in DB) |
| `algorithm_id` format | `inbred_nopca_EIGEN` | `LMM-EXACT-INBRED_noPCA_EIGEN` |

Both encode the same information; the `normalize_algorithm_id()` helper strips the prefix and
uppercases for joining.

## 2. Setup

```{r}
#| label: setup
library(dplyr)
library(data.table)

# Source the shared R library (here::here() finds project root via .git)
Sys.setenv(NEMASCAN_R_LIB = here::here("R"))
source(here::here("R", "setup.R"))
```

```{r}
#| label: paths
# For interactive use, set full paths here:
# existing_assessment_path <- "/full/path/to/simulation_assessment_results.tsv"
# db_assessment_path       <- "/full/path/to/db_simulation_assessment_results.tsv"
# db_dir                   <- "/full/path/to/db"
existing_assessment_path <- "/Users/ryanmckeown/Desktop/nemascan-sims-nf/Analysis_Results-20260213/simulation_assessment_results.tsv"
db_assessment_path       <- "/Users/ryanmckeown/Desktop/nemascan-sims-nf/Analysis_Results-20260213/db_simulation_assessment_results.tsv"
db_dir                   <- "/Users/ryanmckeown/Desktop/nemascan-sims-nf/Analysis_Results-20260213/db"



# Read from Quarto params, fall back to env vars, then interactive vars above
if (!exists("existing_assessment_path") || existing_assessment_path == "") {
  existing_assessment_path <- if (params$existing_assessment != "") {
    params$existing_assessment
  } else {
    Sys.getenv("TEST_EXISTING_ASSESSMENT", unset = "")
  }
}

if (!exists("db_assessment_path") || db_assessment_path == "") {
  db_assessment_path <- if (params$db_assessment != "") {
    params$db_assessment
  } else {
    Sys.getenv("TEST_DB_ASSESSMENT", unset = "")
  }
}

if (!exists("db_dir") || db_dir == "") {
  db_dir <- if (params$db_dir != "") {
    params$db_dir
  } else {
    Sys.getenv("TEST_DB_DIR", unset = "")
  }
}

# Validate
stopifnot(
  "existing_assessment path not set or file missing" =
    existing_assessment_path != "" && file.exists(existing_assessment_path),
  "db_assessment path not set or file missing" =
    db_assessment_path != "" && file.exists(db_assessment_path),
  "db_dir not set or directory missing" =
    db_dir != "" && dir.exists(db_dir)
)
message("Existing assessment: ", existing_assessment_path)
message("DB assessment:       ", db_assessment_path)
message("DB directory:        ", db_dir)
```

## 3. Helper Functions

These are the same helpers used in the test file, adapted for notebook use.

```{r}
#| label: helpers
# Parse legacy assessment TSV (no header, 35-column schema from Assess_Sims.R)
read_existing_assessment <- function(path) {
  col_names <- c("QTL", "Simulated", "Detected", "CHROM", "POS", "RefAllele",
                  "Frequency", "Effect", "Simulated.QTL.VarExp", "log10p", "significant",
                  "startPOS", "peakPOS", "endPOS",
                  "detected.peak", "BETA", "interval.log10p",
                  "peak_id", "interval_size",
                  "interval.var.exp", "interval.Frequency",
                  "top.hit", "nQTL", "simREP", "h2", "maf",
                  "effect_distribution", "strain_set_id",
                  "mode", "type", "threshold", "algorithm_id",
                  "alpha", "ci_size", "snp_grouping")

  df <- tryCatch(
    data.table::fread(path, header = FALSE, col.names = col_names,
                      sep = "\t", na.strings = c("NA", "")) %>% as.data.frame(),
    error = function(e) {
      data.table::fread(path, header = TRUE, sep = "\t",
                        na.strings = c("NA", "")) %>% as.data.frame()
    }
  )

  df$QTL <- as.character(df$QTL)
  df$Simulated <- as.character(df$Simulated)
  df$Detected <- as.character(df$Detected)
  df$startPOS <- as.integer(df$startPOS)
  df$peakPOS <- as.integer(df$peakPOS)
  df$endPOS <- as.integer(df$endPOS)
  df$nQTL <- as.character(df$nQTL)
  df$simREP <- as.character(df$simREP)
  df$h2 <- as.character(df$h2)
  df$maf <- as.character(df$maf)
  df$strain_set_id <- as.character(df$strain_set_id)
  df
}

# Parse DB assessment TSV (no header, 35-column standardized schema)
read_db_assessment <- function(path) {
  col_names <- c("QTL", "Simulated", "Detected", "CHROM", "POS", "RefAllele",
                  "Frequency", "Effect", "Simulated.QTL.VarExp", "log10p", "significant",
                  "BETA", "startPOS", "peakPOS", "endPOS", "detected.peak",
                  "interval.log10p", "interval.var.exp", "interval.Frequency",
                  "peak_id", "interval_size", "top.hit",
                  "nQTL", "simREP", "h2", "maf", "effect_distribution",
                  "strain_set_id", "mode", "type", "threshold", "algorithm_id",
                  "alpha", "ci_size", "snp_grouping")

  df <- tryCatch(
    data.table::fread(path, header = FALSE, col.names = col_names,
                      sep = "\t", na.strings = c("NA", "")) %>% as.data.frame(),
    error = function(e) {
      data.table::fread(path, header = TRUE, sep = "\t",
                        na.strings = c("NA", "")) %>% as.data.frame()
    }
  )

  df$QTL <- as.character(df$QTL)
  df$Simulated <- as.character(df$Simulated)
  df$Detected <- as.character(df$Detected)
  df$startPOS <- as.integer(df$startPOS)
  df$peakPOS <- as.integer(df$peakPOS)
  df$endPOS <- as.integer(df$endPOS)
  df$nQTL <- as.character(df$nQTL)
  df$simREP <- as.character(df$simREP)
  df$h2 <- as.character(df$h2)
  df$maf <- as.character(df$maf)
  df$strain_set_id <- as.character(df$strain_set_id)
  df
}

# Normalize algorithm_id across both paths for joining
# Legacy: "inbred_nopca_EIGEN" → "INBRED_NOPCA_EIGEN"
# DB:     "LMM-EXACT-INBRED_noPCA_EIGEN" → "INBRED_NOPCA_EIGEN"
normalize_algorithm_id <- function(alg_id) {
  toupper(sub("^LMM-EXACT-", "", toupper(alg_id)))
}

# Create a composite key for joining rows across both assessments
make_join_key <- function(df) {
  df %>%
    mutate(
      norm_alg = normalize_algorithm_id(algorithm_id),
      join_key = paste(nQTL, simREP, h2, maf, effect_distribution,
                       strain_set_id, norm_alg, QTL, sep = "|")
    )
}
```

## 4. Load Data

```{r}
#| label: load-data
existing <- read_existing_assessment(existing_assessment_path)
db_assess <- read_db_assessment(db_assessment_path)

message("Existing assessment: ", nrow(existing), " rows x ", ncol(existing), " cols")
message("DB assessment:       ", nrow(db_assess), " rows x ", ncol(db_assess), " cols")
```

Column names side-by-side:

```{r}
#| label: column-preview
data.frame(
  col_index   = seq_along(names(existing)),
  existing    = names(existing),
  db          = names(db_assess),
  same        = names(existing) == names(db_assess)
)
```

Quick look at first few rows of each:

```{r}
#| label: head-preview
head(existing, 3)
head(db_assess, 3)
```

## 5. Row Counts per Mapping

Group by the mapping key (nQTL, simREP, h2, maf, strain_set_id, algorithm) and compare counts.

```{r}
#| label: row-counts
existing_counts <- existing %>%
  mutate(norm_alg = normalize_algorithm_id(algorithm_id)) %>%
  group_by(nQTL, simREP, h2, maf, strain_set_id, norm_alg) %>%
  summarise(n_existing = n(), .groups = "drop")

db_counts <- db_assess %>%
  mutate(norm_alg = normalize_algorithm_id(algorithm_id)) %>%
  group_by(nQTL, simREP, h2, maf, strain_set_id, norm_alg) %>%
  summarise(n_db = n(), .groups = "drop")

stopifnot("existing assessment has no mappings" = nrow(existing_counts) > 0)
stopifnot("DB assessment has no mappings" = nrow(db_counts) > 0)

merged_counts <- full_join(existing_counts, db_counts,
                           by = c("nQTL", "simREP", "h2", "maf", "strain_set_id", "norm_alg"))

row_counts_df <- merged_counts %>%
  mutate(match = n_existing == n_db)
```

```{r}
#| label: row-counts-table

knitr::kable(row_counts_df)
```

```{r}
#| label: row-count-verdict
mismatched <- merged_counts %>% filter(n_existing != n_db | is.na(n_existing) | is.na(n_db))
if (nrow(mismatched) == 0) {
  message("PASS: All mappings have matching row counts.")
} else {
  message("FAIL: ", nrow(mismatched), " mappings have mismatched row counts:")
  print(mismatched)
}
```

## 6. Detection Outcomes

Compare `Simulated` / `Detected` flags for simulated QTLs across both paths.

```{r}
#| label: detection-outcomes
existing_sim <- existing %>%
  filter(Simulated == "TRUE") %>%
  make_join_key()

db_sim <- db_assess %>%
  filter(Simulated == "TRUE") %>%
  make_join_key()

message("Simulated QTLs — existing: ", nrow(existing_sim), ", DB: ", nrow(db_sim))
```

```{r}
#| label: detection-join
if (nrow(existing_sim) > 0 && nrow(db_sim) > 0) {
  merged_detection <- inner_join(
    existing_sim %>% select(join_key, Detected_existing = Detected),
    db_sim %>% select(join_key, Detected_db = Detected),
    by = "join_key"
  )

  message("Joined rows: ", nrow(merged_detection))

  mismatches <- merged_detection %>% filter(Detected_existing != Detected_db)

  if (nrow(mismatches) == 0) {
    message("PASS: Detection outcomes match for all ", nrow(merged_detection), " simulated QTLs.")
  } else {
    message("FAIL: ", nrow(mismatches), " detection mismatches:")
    print(mismatches)
  }
} else {
  message("SKIP: No simulated QTLs found in one or both paths.")
}
```

## 7. Threshold Validation

Query the DB for BF and EIGEN threshold parameters and verify against the expected formula:

- **BF**: `-log10(alpha / n_markers)`
- **EIGEN**: `-log10(alpha / n_independent_tests)`

```{r}
#| label: threshold-validation
marker_sets <- list_marker_sets(db_dir)
message("Marker sets in DB: ", nrow(marker_sets))
marker_sets
```

```{r}
#| label: threshold-check
all_pass <- TRUE
for (i in seq_len(nrow(marker_sets))) {
  pop <- marker_sets$population[i]
  maf_val <- marker_sets$maf[i]

  thresh_params <- get_threshold_params(pop, maf_val, 0.05, db_dir)

  # BF threshold: -log10(0.05 / n_markers)
  expected_bf <- -log10(0.05 / thresh_params$n_markers)
  bf_match <- isTRUE(all.equal(thresh_params$bf_threshold, expected_bf))

  # EIGEN threshold: -log10(0.05 / n_independent_tests)
  if (!is.na(thresh_params$n_independent_tests)) {
    expected_eigen <- -log10(0.05 / thresh_params$n_independent_tests)
    eigen_match <- isTRUE(all.equal(thresh_params$eigen_threshold, expected_eigen))
  } else {
    expected_eigen <- NA
    eigen_match <- TRUE
  }

  status <- if (bf_match && eigen_match) "PASS" else "FAIL"
  if (!bf_match || !eigen_match) all_pass <- FALSE

  message(sprintf("[%s] %s (maf=%.2f): BF=%.4f (expected %.4f), EIGEN=%s (expected %s)",
                  status, pop, maf_val,
                  thresh_params$bf_threshold, expected_bf,
                  ifelse(is.na(thresh_params$eigen_threshold), "NA", sprintf("%.4f", thresh_params$eigen_threshold)),
                  ifelse(is.na(expected_eigen), "NA", sprintf("%.4f", expected_eigen))))
}

if (all_pass) message("\nPASS: All thresholds match expected formulas.")
```

## 8. QTL Interval Boundaries

Join detected QTLs from both paths and compare `startPOS`, `peakPOS`, `endPOS`.

```{r}
#| label: interval-boundaries
existing_detected <- existing %>%
  filter(Detected == "TRUE", !is.na(startPOS)) %>%
  make_join_key()

db_detected <- db_assess %>%
  filter(Detected == "TRUE", !is.na(startPOS)) %>%
  make_join_key()
```

### Existing QTL Workflow Detected
```{r}
#| label: existing-detected 

knitr::kable(existing_detected)
```

### Database QTL Workflow Detected

```{r}
#| label: db-detected 

knitr::kable(db_detected)
```

```{r}
#| label: interval-compare
if (nrow(existing_detected) > 0 && nrow(db_detected) > 0) {
  merged_intervals <- inner_join(
    existing_detected %>%
      select(join_key, startPOS_e = startPOS, peakPOS_e = peakPOS, endPOS_e = endPOS),
    db_detected %>%
      select(join_key, startPOS_d = startPOS, peakPOS_d = peakPOS, endPOS_d = endPOS),
    by = "join_key"
  )

  message("Joined detected QTLs: ", nrow(merged_intervals))

  if (nrow(merged_intervals) > 0) {
    merged_intervals <- merged_intervals %>%
      mutate(
        start_match = startPOS_e == startPOS_d,
        peak_match  = peakPOS_e == peakPOS_d,
        end_match   = endPOS_e == endPOS_d
      )

    # Show the comparison table
    merged_intervals

    n_start_mismatch <- sum(!merged_intervals$start_match)
    n_peak_mismatch  <- sum(!merged_intervals$peak_match)
    n_end_mismatch   <- sum(!merged_intervals$end_match)

    if (n_start_mismatch + n_peak_mismatch + n_end_mismatch == 0) {
      message("PASS: All interval boundaries match.")
    } else {
      message(sprintf("FAIL: Mismatches — startPOS: %d, peakPOS: %d, endPOS: %d",
                      n_start_mismatch, n_peak_mismatch, n_end_mismatch))
      merged_intervals %>% filter(!start_match | !peak_match | !end_match)
    }
  }
} else {
  message("SKIP: No detected QTLs with intervals in one or both paths.")
}
```

## 9. Detected/Simulated Counts

Per-mapping comparison of how many QTLs were detected and how many were simulated.

```{r}
#| label: count-comparison
count_by_mapping <- function(df) {
  df %>%
    mutate(norm_alg = normalize_algorithm_id(algorithm_id)) %>%
    group_by(nQTL, simREP, h2, maf, strain_set_id, norm_alg) %>%
    summarise(
      n_detected = sum(Detected == "TRUE", na.rm = TRUE),
      n_simulated = sum(Simulated == "TRUE", na.rm = TRUE),
      .groups = "drop"
    )
}

existing_counts <- count_by_mapping(existing)
db_counts <- count_by_mapping(db_assess)

stopifnot("existing assessment has no mappings" = nrow(existing_counts) > 0)
stopifnot("DB assessment has no mappings" = nrow(db_counts) > 0)

merged <- inner_join(
  existing_counts, db_counts,
  by = c("nQTL", "simREP", "h2", "maf", "strain_set_id", "norm_alg"),
  suffix = c("_existing", "_db")
)

merged
```

```{r}
#| label: count-verdict
detect_mismatches <- merged %>% filter(n_detected_existing != n_detected_db)
sim_mismatches    <- merged %>% filter(n_simulated_existing != n_simulated_db)

if (nrow(detect_mismatches) == 0 && nrow(sim_mismatches) == 0) {
  message("PASS: Detected and simulated counts match across all ", nrow(merged), " mappings.")
} else {
  if (nrow(detect_mismatches) > 0) {
    message("FAIL: ", nrow(detect_mismatches), " mappings have mismatched detected counts:")
    print(detect_mismatches)
  }
  if (nrow(sim_mismatches) > 0) {
    message("FAIL: ", nrow(sim_mismatches), " mappings have mismatched simulated counts:")
    print(sim_mismatches)
  }
}
```

## 10. Output File Checks

Verify both assessment files exist and are non-empty.

```{r}
#| label: file-checks
existing_size <- file.info(existing_assessment_path)$size
db_size       <- file.info(db_assessment_path)$size

file_info <- data.frame(
  path = c(existing_assessment_path, db_assessment_path),
  exists = c(file.exists(existing_assessment_path), file.exists(db_assessment_path)),
  size_bytes = c(existing_size, db_size)
)
file_info

stopifnot("existing assessment file missing" = file.exists(existing_assessment_path))
stopifnot("DB assessment file missing"       = file.exists(db_assessment_path))
stopifnot("existing assessment is empty"      = existing_size > 0)
stopifnot("DB assessment is empty"            = db_size > 0)

message("PASS: Both files exist and are non-empty.")
```

## 11. var.exp NA Validation

The DB path has no genotype matrix available during import, so `interval.var.exp` should be `NA`
for all rows.

```{r}
#| label: varexp-check
if ("interval.var.exp" %in% names(db_assess)) {
  non_na_count <- sum(!is.na(db_assess$interval.var.exp))

  if (non_na_count == 0) {
    message("PASS: interval.var.exp is all NA in DB path (", nrow(db_assess), " rows checked).")
  } else {
    message("FAIL: ", non_na_count, " rows have non-NA interval.var.exp in DB path.")
    db_assess %>% filter(!is.na(interval.var.exp)) %>% select(QTL, algorithm_id, interval.var.exp)
  }
} else {
  message("SKIP: interval.var.exp column not present in DB assessment.")
}
```
